The '''Fizz-Pop Cap (with foil cluster)''' was an accoutrement worn by [[Squib/Legends|Squib]] [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomats]] who represented the Squib [[King/Legends|King]].<ref name="SH" /> Sometime between [[0 ABY/Legends|0]] and [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]],<ref name="date">[[Scavenger Hunt (adventure)|''Scavenger Hunt'']] takes place at an unspecified point after the destruction of the first [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]], which occurred in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', and before the assignment of [[Carlist Rieekan/Legends|Carlist Rieekan]] to [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]], which occurred in [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]] according to ''The New Essential Chronology''.</ref> [[Spilferithimus-narlamos]]—nicknamed Spilfer—wore such a cap when he was sent by King [[Ebareebaveebeedee]] from the Squib [[Momship]] ''[[Thrifty]]'' to join a band of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] operatives. The Rebels were on a [[Mission to destroy Elusive|mission]] to destroy [[Elusive (transport)|''Elusive'']], an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Imperial Armored Transport|Armored Transport]] that held sensitive information about Rebel activities under control of the Rebels' [[Dankayo Base]], located on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dankayo/Legends|Dankayo]]. However, only the Squibs knew the [[Starship/Legends|starship's]] current whereabouts, coordinates they promised to trade only after the Rebels helped Spilfer steal a [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity well projector]] from the [[Paradise system/Legends|Paradise system]], home to the Squibs' [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] rivals, the [[Ugor/Legends|Ugors]].<ref name="SH" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Fizz-Pop Cap is mentioned in the description of Spilferithimus-narlamos in the [[adventure]] [[Scavenger Hunt (adventure)|''Scavenger Hunt'']], written by [[Brad Freeman]] and published by [[West End Games]] in [[1989]] as part of ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. The adventure includes an illustration by [[John Robinson]], which depicts Spilfer wearing a hat that sits between his tufted ears and features a broad, flat, rounded bill and a collection of keyhole-shaped objects on the front. Nevertheless, the adventure's use of ''cap'' is ambiguous about whether the term refers to Spilfer's hat or some other element of his costume.<ref name="SH" />
